source: FDA 2024 ADC Guidance; gemtuzumab ozogamicin/inotuzumab ozogamicin BLA reviews
description: Calicheamicin causes DNA double-strand breaks via oxidative radical mechanism. pH-sensitive hydrazone linker is relatively unstable (plasma half-life 1-2 days), driving significant free calicheamicin exposure. Kupffer cell uptake delivers payload to hepatic sinusoidal endothelium. Primary DLTs: severe thrombocytopenia and hepatic SOS/VOD.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Even moderate preclinical thrombocytopenia for this class should trigger adversity flag. 77-99% Grade 3+ incidence clinically.
expected_findings.rationale: DNA damage to megakaryocytes. Most severe thrombocytopenia of any ADC payload class.
expected_findings.translation_gap: 77-99% Grade 3+ incidence clinically. Even moderate preclinical findings are significant.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any histopathological SOS evidence = adverse. Fatal SOS reported with inotuzumab ozogamicin.
expected_findings.rationale: Target-independent (confirmed with non-binding control ADC). Kupffer cell uptake delivers calicheamicin to hepatic sinusoidal endothelium.
expected_findings.translation_gap: SOS underestimated in some preclinical programs; fatal SOS reported in inotuzumab trials.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Severe hypocellularity or aplasia = adverse. More severe than microtubule inhibitor ADCs.
expected_findings.rationale: DNA double-strand breaks in hematopoietic progenitors. More severe than microtubule inhibitor ADCs.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any multilineage cytopenia with clinical consequences = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Multilineage pattern distinguishes DNA-damaging ADCs from MMAE (primarily neutropenia) or MMAF (primarily thrombocytopenia).

expected_findings.description: GI mucosal injury (more severe than microtubule inhibitor ADCs)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Mucosal necrosis or hemorrhagic enteritis = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: More severe than microtubule inhibitor ADCs; stomatitis more prominent.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Elevated bilirubin + hepatomegaly = SOS sentinel. Any SOS histopathological evidence = adverse.
expected_findings.rationale: Bilirubin elevation with transaminase increase is the sentinel laboratory pattern for SOS.
expected_findings.linker_note: Hydrazone linker instability is the primary driver of off-target toxicity. Premature calicheamicin release produces systemic exposure that amplifies all findings. Free calicheamicin AUC / total ADC AUC ratio >10% indicates significant systemic payload release.
